goal: Flag-reduction campaign to ZERO registry rows (len(flagregistry.All)==0) via design patterns (config-as-code policy.json, DI seams, cobra flags, split-const IPC). Baseline at RELAUNCH (2026-06-22, off hardened main dd078ff6): 35 rows / 18 live feature flags. flag-campaign-8 wave-1 (salvaged in PR #211) ALREADY DELETED 13 rows (5 dead + COMPOSE_PHASES + 7 of 8 tombstones) — do NOT re-attempt those, they are gone. The ONLY remaining tombstone is EVOLVE_POLICY_BYPASS (deliberately deferred: its env bridge is a live operator escape hatch, so it needs a proper --bypass-policy cobra flag before the row can go — see w1-policy-bypass-cli). Each cycle DELETES the os.Getenv/envchain read so the value flows from a Config struct loaded once; NEVER hide a config read behind split-const (cycle-8 anti-gaming rule). VERIFY-BEFORE-DELETE (HARD): before deleting any registry row, grep-prove zero os.Getenv readers AND zero text-surface refs (skills/agents/.github/*.sh + go string-literals); clean surface refs first. ROW-DELETE + PREDICATE (HARD — the cycle-10 lesson): converting a reader is NOT the deliverable; DELETING the registry row is. A cycle that refactors a reader (split-const/policy field) but leaves the row shipped a PASS with zero progress (relaunch cycle 10: rows 35->35). So (a) every cycle MUST delete the registry_table.go row(s) for the flag(s) it owns, and (b) the cycle's OWN acs predicate MUST assert flagregistry.Lookup("EVOLVE_X") returns false for each — a predicate that only checks the conversion (e.g. the split-const exists) is INSUFFICIENT. ENFORCED: go/acs/regression/flagprogress (active under EVOLVE_FLAG_CAMPAIGN=1) FAILS the per-cycle audit gate unless len(flagregistry.All) strictly DECREASED vs HEAD — a cosmetic refactor that nets zero deletions cannot ship. A flag with MULTIPLE readers across files/processes (e.g. PHASE_RECOVERY) needs ALL readers converted in the SAME cycle before the row can go; do the whole flag or none. CEILING DECOUPLING (HARD): do NOT edit FlagCeiling or LiveFeatureFlagCeiling in registry_ceiling_test.go — deletions only lower len(All) below the ceiling so all tests still pass; the operator ratchets both consts at each integration PR (this removes the one line every cycle would collide on, making concurrency safe). The anti-regression gate is live: go/acs/regression/flagceiling fails if live count rises vs main, and TestRegistry_LiveFeatureFlagCeiling caps live at 18. Per-flag targets in knowledge-base/research/flag-reduction-47-to-0-design-2026-06-21.md, but three design entries were STALE — the corrections are folded into the cycle output_contracts below. DOC PRESERVATION (HARD): never delete, move, or overwrite any knowledge-base/research/*.md design or research doc (or this plan) — they are the campaign's design-of-record; flag work touches only go/ source, skills/, agents/, .github/. CLAUDE-ONLY: route every phase to claude (codex hard-capped till Jun 25, agy on quota); the cross-family auditor!=builder floor is a preference, not a ship-gate — opus-auditor vs sonnet-builder model separation satisfies integrity.

research.summary: Design-of-record (ultracode deep-dive). Remaining waves after the salvage: w1 (POLICY_BYPASS CLI conversion — the deferred 14th flag), w2 (4 sub-cycles: fleet-IPC, phaserecovery-IPC, paths-config, profile-seams), w3 (cli-config + bootstrap-locators), w4 (THE load-bearing config.Load env->RolloutInput DTO inversion subsuming 12 flags), w5 (flagreaders protocolAllowlist for the irreducible prompt-IPC literal EVOLVE_REFLECTION_JOURNAL + assert len(All)==0). Every one of the 35 current rows is covered exactly once across these cycles (verified 2026-06-22). CORRECTIONS already folded in: (1) EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY is read via os.Getenv in the observer SUBPROCESS (cli/phasecmd/phase_observer.go:118), NOT config.go applyEnv — split-const IPC, NOT a config.Load field; handled in w2-phaserecovery-ipc, NOT w4. (2) EVOLVE_REFLECTION_JOURNAL is a markdown prompt-IPC literal (~18 agents/*.md sites) that cannot be split-const'd — it gets a justified protocolAllowlist entry in w5, not a deletion. (3) bootstrap locators PLUGIN_ROOT/PROJECT_ROOT are core-infra Active rows — they reach zero via cobra-flag-primary + split-const env fallback in w3-bootstrap-locators. file-disjoint cycles run concurrently; the shared registry_table.go edits 3-way-merge cleanly once the ceiling-const collision is removed (see CEILING DECOUPLING).

cycles.output_contract: Convert the LAST tombstone EVOLVE_POLICY_BYPASS to a proper cobra flag, then delete its row. The PhaseRequest.BypassPolicy bool field already exists; the env BRIDGE (cycleEnv["EVOLVE_POLICY_BYPASS"]=="1") in cmd_cycle.go and cmd_loop.go is the only live reader and is a deliberate operator escape hatch that MUST NOT be silently dropped. Add a persistent --bypass-policy bool flag on the cycle and loop commands (default false) that sets BypassPolicy; keep req.Env precedence on the cycle dispatch path if present. Remove the cycleEnv env-bridge reads. Then delete the EVOLVE_POLICY_BYPASS row from registry_table.go. Update go/acs/cycle10/predicates_test.go: C10_004 and C10_006 currently EXCLUDE POLICY_BYPASS — add it back to the asserted-absent sets now that the bridge is gone. Verify-before-delete: grep proves zero os.Getenv/cycleEnv readers and zero surface refs remain. Do NOT edit FlagCeiling/LiveFeatureFlagCeiling.

Reclassify EVOLVE_FLEET, EVOLVE_FLEET_SCOPE, EVOLVE_WORKTREE_ROOT to split-const IPC consts at one def site each (// SSOT IPC-protocol-allowed) and route EVERY reader through the const (no bare literal). These are cross-process coordination signals (parent sets them in child cmd.Env), so they are protocol, not operator dials — the split-const makes that explicit. Rows leave the registry. Do NOT edit ceiling consts.

CORRECTED INVENTORY (cycle-10 shipped a no-op because it converted only 1 of these). EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY has FOUR real readers across three process contexts — ALL must be converted in THIS cycle before the row can go: (1) config.go:518 `env["EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY"]` → the canonical config.Load/applyEnv read into cfg.PhaseRecovery; pull this field's resolution to policy.RecoveryConfig() at wireOrchestratorDeps (do the PHASE_RECOVERY slice of the w4 inversion here so w4 does NOT re-add it). (2) bridge/fatalpane.go:47 `lookupEnv(deps,"EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY")` and (3) adapters/observer/core_adapter.go:144 `envGet("EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY")` → in-process bridge/observer seams; route through the resolved cfg/policy value via their existing DI seams. (4) phasecmd/phase_observer.go:118 `os.Getenv("EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY")` → the observer SUBPROCESS; parent injects the resolved stage via a split-const IPC env var (// SSOT IPC-protocol-allowed), subprocess reads ONLY through that const (NOT a bare concat that still os.Getenv's the same name — that is the cycle-10 anti-pattern). Then DELETE the registry_table.go row, and write an acs predicate asserting flagregistry.Lookup("EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY")==false. The flagprogress gate verifies len(All) dropped. Do NOT edit ceiling consts.

READER INVENTORY (verified 2026-06-23 — convert ALL sites then delete each row): EVOLVE_CLI_MAX_CONCURRENT_CODEX is DEAD — its ONLY occurrence in go/ is the registry row itself (0 readers, 0 tests); DELETE the row only, do NOT add a ConcurrencyConfig field nobody reads. EVOLVE_PHASE_ROOTS is read via the single SSOT const rootsEnv="EVOLVE_PHASE_ROOTS" (phasespec/mergedcatalog.go:18) → convert that one const (policy.PathsConfig or split-const) and route readers through it. EVOLVE_ACS_GO_TIMEOUT_S, EVOLVE_KB_SEARCH_PATHS -> policy.json (ACSConfig/PathsConfig); EVOLVE_MODEL_CATALOG_DIR (2 readers) -> DI setter. Delete env reads; rewrite the acs cycle3/cycle5 predicates in the same diff if they reference these. Pattern: follow ClassifyPolicy/CatalogPolicy at policy.go ~817/838. Each removed flag needs a Lookup-absent acs predicate; flagprogress verifies len(All) drops. Do NOT edit ceiling consts.

EVOLVE_PERSONA_OVERRIDE + EVOLVE_PROFILE_DIR -> cobra flags (keep req.Env precedence on the cycle path ship.go:110); EVOLVE_SYSTEM_PROMPT -> profile SSOT (drop os.Getenv, keep per-dispatch req.Env tier). Do NOT edit ceiling consts.

EVOLVE_CLI (global), EVOLVE_CLI_HEALTH, EVOLVE_INTENT_DELTA, EVOLVE_STRICT_AUDIT -> policy.json typed fields; thread on ALL construction paths (specrunner/registrar minted, resume.go:251, cycle ship). Delete env reads; e2e harness writes policy.json not ExtraEnv. Do NOT edit ceiling consts.

LARGEST + HIGHEST-RISK CYCLE (reader counts verified 2026-06-23 — flagreaders will FAIL if ANY literal site is missed, so route EVERY site through the per-flag SSOT const): EVOLVE_PROJECT_ROOT=14 readers, EVOLVE_PLUGIN_ROOT=7, EVOLVE_GO_BIN=3, EVOLVE_WORKTREE_BASE=2 (~26 sites total). Define ONE split-const per flag (// SSOT bootstrap-cli-flag, e.g. const envProjectRoot = "EVOLVE_"+"PROJECT_ROOT") at the resolver and replace ALL bare literals with the const; add a cobra flag primary with the split-const env as fallback; GO_BIN re-injects into child cmd.Env. EVOLVE_PROMPTS_DIR (2 readers) -> split-const IPC (load-bearing e2e handoff), behavior kept but its row leaves the registry via the // SSOT const. NOTE: PLUGIN_ROOT/PROJECT_ROOT are the core-infra rows — once they resolve via cobra-flag + split-const, drop them from registry_livefeatureflags_test.go coreInfraExpected too. If 26 sites is too large for one clean cycle, do ONE flag per cycle (each still nets a row deletion → satisfies flagprogress); never leave a half-converted flag (a missed literal without a row FAILs flagreaders). Each removed flag needs a Lookup-absent predicate. Do NOT edit ceiling consts.

THE LOAD-BEARING ATOMIC DIFF. Invert config.Load(env) -> config.Load(RolloutInput DTO) built at wireOrchestratorDeps from pol.{OperatorRouting,Gates,Router,Sandbox}Config(); config stays a leaf (no policy import), reuse the unexported parsers. Subsumes the applyEnv flags: EVOLVE_DYNAMIC_ROUTING, EVOLVE_ROUTING_MODE, EVOLVE_MANDATORY_PHASES, EVOLVE_CONDITIONAL_MANDATORY, EVOLVE_MAX_OPTIONAL_INSERTIONS, EVOLVE_COMMIT_EVIDENCE, EVOLVE_PHASE_IO, EVOLVE_SANDBOX, EVOLVE_USE_PHASE_REGISTRY + delete the *_DIR_OVERRIDE branches from paths.Resolve (EVOLVE_LEDGER_OVERRIDE, EVOLVE_PROFILES_DIR_OVERRIDE, EVOLVE_ADAPTERS_DIR_OVERRIDE). NOTE: PHASE_RECOVERY is NOT here (handled as IPC in w2-phaserecovery-ipc). Ship config_rollout_parity_test.go proving byte-identical RoutingConfig for both Load callers. applyEnv has ZERO EVOLVE_ reads after. 12 rows leave the registry. Do NOT edit ceiling consts.

TERMINAL. Add a protocolAllowlist to the flagreaders guard for EVOLVE_REFLECTION_JOURNAL (markdown prompt-IPC, ~18 agents/*.md sites, cannot split-const) with a one-line justification; wire policy.WorkflowConfig().ReflectionJournal to gate the env injection; KEEP the literal in agents/*.md. Assert len(flagregistry.All)==0 and every remaining production EVOLVE_ literal is a // SSOT split-const or a justified allowlist entry. Do NOT edit ceiling consts here — the operator sets FlagCeiling=LiveFeatureFlagCeiling=0 at the final integration PR.
